SUBJECTIVE:
Milo, 4y MN Labrador. Diarrhoea since yesterday morning, first noted about 6am yesterday, 6-7 episodes yesterday and 3 this morning. Stool watery with mucus, tiny red streak 1x last night. 1 vomit yesterday afternoon, yellow/foamy, none overnight. Appetite lower, drinking slightly more, slightly quieter but still waggy/wanting to go out, urgency overnight with no house accident. Possible dietary indiscretion at park (sausage/bread), daycare contact with dog with loose stools, Pro-Kolin approx 2 ml BID...

OBJECTIVE:
Bright, responsive, friendly, slightly quieter than typical. Wt 28.4 kg. T 38.7C, HR 108, RR 28. MM pink/moist. CRT<2. Mild cranial abdo discomfort on deep palpation, no obvious mass, no severe guarding.

ASSESSMENT:
Acute gastroenteritis, likely dietary indiscretion.

PLAN:
Maropitant 1 mg/kg SC in clinic. Pro-Kolin 5 ml PO q8h x3 days. Bland diet small frequent meals x3 days, then transition to normal food over 2-3d if stools improve. Encourage water. Avoid high-fat treats and no new foods this week. Short lead walks only today. Monitor for repeated vomiting, unable to keep water down, increasing blood in stool, marked lethargy, abdominal pain, or no improvement by 48h. Nurse progress call tomorrow at 15:30.
